title: I Monetized This Channel in 12 Days To Prove It’s Not Luck - Just Copy Me

transcript.text: In just 12 days, and with only three videos, I monetized this YouTube channel. And in case you're wondering, yes, I used AI to create the script, I used AI to create the audio, and I used AI to create some of the visuals. So, in this video, I'm going to show you the step-by-step process on exactly how you can do this yourself. But first, let me show you the numbers. So, I started this YouTube channel on May 4th. That day, I posted one single video. Then on May 5th, I posted the second video. And in the next 3 days, I was already getting 400 views per day. If I keep scrolling, you can see that within a week, I was already getting tens of thousands of views. Now, if I go up to custom, and I click on May 18th, which is the day I was officially monetized, you can see that this channel has made $2,948 in just 23 days. And if I scroll down, you'll see my RPM is $3.52, which if you don't know, RPM just means how much YouTube is going to pay me per 1,000 views. So, every 1,000 views I get, YouTube pays me $3.52. Okay? So, today is June 10th, and this month so far, the channel has made $1,443. If I go over to my most popular video, it has 743,000 views, and that's paid me $1,386. Now, keep in mind, I posted this video before I was monetized. So, a lot of those views I was not paid for, sadly. But when the channel was finally monetized, I started making over $50 every single day. So, yeah, those are the analytics of the channel. Now, something crazy that you might not be taking into consideration is that I've only posted seven videos. To create these seven videos, I only paid $175. Again, I made the majority of it with AI besides the editing. So, with $175, I made $2,948. Now, you could say I got lucky, but I also monetized this YouTube channel in 9 days. This other YouTube channel, I did it in 15 days. This one, I did it in 28 days. And this dead monetized YouTube channel was revived in just 13 days. This does not happen by accident. This happened because I follow a very strict set of rules. And rule number one is to only target ghost niches. Now, ghost niches is the complete opposite of what every other YouTube coach is going to tell you to do, right? They're going to tell you to find a successful YouTube channel and to just copy that niche. To copy his video ideas. To copy his thumbnails. But that doesn't work, right? YouTube has changed a lot. They've done a lot of updates. They have their own AI detectors and they know if you grabbed someone's YouTube script and reworded it, right? They're not stupid. They also know when you're copying someone's video ideas. You can't do that anymore with YouTube automation. That sentence coming from me is crazy, okay? Because I started in 2018 launching celebrity news YouTube channels. And guess what? What we did at that time was kind of just copy channels. I don't like to bring this up, but I was even in the Moist Critical video because I was copying other people. Yeah, that was that was random. And listen, in doing that strategy, I made millions of dollars. And again, yes, millions. One single celebrity news YouTube channel paid me over $1 million. One single YouTube video paid me over $98,000 and it was about the Johnny Depp and Amber Heard court case. That was kind of my whole business model. Was copying what other people were posting, which explained why when I tried starting new channels with that same strategy, it would take me a very long time to get any other YouTube channel to work. But the last five YouTube channels I launched, which are the ones I just showed you, those have been back-to-back. And again, I did this by following ghost niches. These are niches that nobody else has posted on YouTube. If you sit back and and you look at YouTube, [music] you're going to realize that so many topics haven't been covered. You're going to realize that if you just don't do what everyone else is doing and post something that's never been posted on YouTube, again, not meaning original, just that nobody has done it, it's going to work. For example, news about gas prices, nobody was doing it, I did it, it worked. News about real estate in Florida, news about factory shutting down, biography videos about country music artists, videos covering the WNBA. What else did I do? I guess golf. I did golf, too.
